variants.global: I return to a hard task after a short walk instead of abandoning it.
variants.ghana: Tough task plus brief walk brings me back to try again.
variants.west_africa: Hard job gets another round after a stroll.

variants.global: Heavy physical work leads me to drink water and sit before the next chore.
variants.ghana: Big exertion gets water plus sit before round two.
variants.west_africa: After hard labor I hydrate and rest briefly.

variants.global: I push to finish a boring but important task the same day I started.
variants.ghana: Dull-but-needed work closes same day it opens.
variants.west_africa: Important boring jobs do not cross midnight idle.

variants.global: I notice muscle soreness and lighten the next task on purpose.
variants.ghana: Sore muscles trigger a lighter next load by choice.
variants.west_africa: Body ache steers next task size down.

variants.global: Mental fatigue makes me switch to a simple hands task for a while.
variants.ghana: Brain tired sends me to easy hand work awhile.
variants.west_africa: Thinking drain moves me to plain manual chores.

variants.global: I split a huge cleaning job across several days on purpose.
variants.ghana: Massive cleaning spreads across planned days.

variants.global: I sleep longer after an unusually demanding day.
variants.ghana: Extra-hard day extends my sleep the night after.
variants.west_africa: Big demand day lengthens next sleep.

variants.global: I take notes during long talks so my mind can rest between points.
variants.ghana: Long speech gets note fragments so my mind breathes.
variants.west_africa: Extended talk pairs with jotting breaks for me.

variants.global: I set a visible timer to work in focused bursts on dull tasks.
variants.ghana: Boring work runs in timed visible bursts.

variants.global: I stop work for food before I feel shaky.
variants.ghana: Meal breaks come before shaky hunger.

variants.global: I celebrate small finished pieces of a long project aloud or in text to someone.
variants.ghana: Tiny milestones get a spoken or text cheer to another person.

variants.global: After conflict talk, I need quiet alone time before the next social task.
variants.ghana: Hot words exchange needs solo quiet before next people task.

variants.global: A hard failed attempt makes me shelve the task for days.
variants.ghana: Tough fail shelves the job many days.

variants.global: I jump straight into the next heavy chore without water or sit.
variants.ghana: Back-to-back heavy chores without pause is my pattern.
variants.west_africa: No sit or sip between hard rounds.

variants.global: Boring important tasks often spill to another day unfinished.
variants.ghana: Dull needed work crosses days open.

variants.global: I ignore early muscle soreness and repeat the same heavy load.
variants.ghana: Early soreness ignored; same load repeated.
variants.west_africa: Soreness does not shrink next task.

variants.global: Mental fatigue makes me stop all work rather than switch task type.
variants.ghana: Brain tired halts everything.
variants.west_africa: Think-tired ends the work block fully.

variants.global: I try to finish huge cleaning in one long push.
variants.ghana: Whole clean in one marathon session is my pull.

variants.global: Demanding day rarely changes how long I sleep.
variants.ghana: Hard day does not stretch night sleep.

variants.global: I rely on memory alone through long talks without jotting.
variants.ghana: Long talks need no notes from me.

variants.global: Dull tasks run without timers until something interrupts.
variants.ghana: No burst timers on boring work.

variants.global: I work through hunger until shaking or headache appears.
variants.ghana: Hunger ignored until body signals hard.
variants.west_africa: Food waits for strong body complaint.

variants.global: I move on to the next task without noting what I finished.
variants.ghana: Finished slices pass without acknowledgment.

variants.global: After conflict talk I go straight into the next crowded task.
variants.ghana: Argument then immediate crowd task is fine for me.
variants.west_africa: No solo buffer needed after tense talk.

variants.global: A few minutes with my eyes closed rarely changes how tired I feel.
variants.ghana: Short quiet with my eyes shut seldom shifts my tired level.
variants.west_africa: A brief eye rest rarely lifts my energy.

variants.global: I understand a new physical skill faster when someone shows me once.
variants.ghana: Seeing a move once beats hearing many words for my body learning.
variants.west_africa: Demo-first beats long verbal-only coaching for my hands.

variants.global: Spoken steps alone are enough for me to copy a simple new routine.
variants.ghana: Voice-only instructions carry simple new routines for me.
variants.west_africa: Hearing the order is enough without pictures.

variants.global: I repeat a new phrase aloud several times to lock it in.
variants.ghana: Fresh phrases get loud repeats to stick.

variants.global: I draw a quick map or diagram to explain where something is.
variants.ghana: Place explanation gets a fast sketch from me.

variants.global: I learn from a mistake if the wrong outcome is shown clearly.
variants.ghana: Clear wrong result teaches me fast.

variants.global: I ask the teacher or guide to slow down when hands and words move too fast together.
variants.ghana: Hands plus words too fast triggers a slow request from me.
variants.west_africa: I request slower combo demo when rushed.

variants.global: I tie a new name to a face feature so I recall it next time.
variants.ghana: Face detail anchors new names for me.

variants.global: I use objects around me as props when telling a story.
variants.ghana: Storytelling grabs nearby objects as examples.

variants.global: Long written instructions tire me; short bullet lists work better.
variants.ghana: Paragraph manuals drain me; bullets help.
variants.west_africa: Wall-of-text rules lose me; lists win.

variants.global: I practice a new dance or sport move in slow motion first.
variants.ghana: Fresh moves start in slow motion.

variants.global: I learn a group game faster after one practice round than after only rules talk.
variants.ghana: One trial round beats rules lecture for group games.
variants.west_africa: Play round teaches group rules faster for me.

variants.global: Charts with colors help me compare amounts at a glance.
variants.ghana: Color bars make amount gaps obvious to my eyes.

variants.global: Watching once is not enough; I need several repeats before my body copies.
variants.ghana: Single demo is too thin; repeats needed for my body.

variants.global: Spoken steps alone are not enough; I need a picture or gesture.
variants.ghana: Voice-only is thin; picture or hand cue needed.
variants.west_africa: Words alone miss for new routines.

variants.global: I lock new phrases silently in my head without saying them aloud.
variants.ghana: Silent mental loop stores new wording.
variants.west_africa: No loud repeats needed for phrases.

variants.global: I describe a place with words only and skip drawing.
variants.ghana: Word picture without sketch is my habit.

variants.global: Mistakes confuse me unless someone walks through the right steps slowly.
variants.ghana: Errors need slow correct walkthrough.

variants.global: Fast hands with fast talk during demo feels fine to me.
variants.ghana: Rapid demo pace matches my learning.

variants.global: I recall new names without linking them to face details.
variants.ghana: Names stick without feature tricks.

variants.global: I tell stories with words only and rarely point to objects.
variants.ghana: Object-free storytelling is my style.
variants.west_africa: Props seldom appear in my tales.

variants.global: Long written instructions feel clearer than very short bullets.
variants.ghana: Full paragraphs beat ultra-short bullets for me.

variants.global: I practice new moves at full speed from the first try.
variants.ghana: Day-one new moves run full tempo.

variants.global: Rules talk alone is enough for me before a group game starts.
variants.ghana: Pre-game lecture carries me without practice round.

variants.global: Color charts feel optional; numbers alone are enough.
variants.ghana: Numeric tables beat color bars for me.

variants.global: Written checklist beats live demo for my learning style.
variants.ghana: Paper checklist tops live show for me.
